items.text:
• Configuration Reference — Complete config schema
  • Commands — Detailed command reference
  • Architecture — Runner system and implementation details

items.title: Directory Structure
items.text: Understanding the workspace and release directory layout in fmd.

items.title: Overview
items.text:
fmd organizes deployments in a structured workspace with versioned releases and persistent data separation.

~/frappe/sites/<site>/
├── workspace/
│   ├── frappe-bench → release_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS  (symlink to current)
│   ├── deployment-data/        (persistent across releases)
│   │   ├── sites/              (DB, files, configs)
│   │   ├── config/             (supervisor configs)
│   │   └── logs/               (application logs)
│   ├── release_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS/  (each release is isolated)
│   │   ├── apps/
│   │   ├── env/                (release-scoped Python venv)
│   │   ├── .uv/                (UV package cache, per-release)
│   │   ├── .fnm/               (Node.js runtime, per-release)
│   │   ├── sites → ../deployment-data/sites  (symlink)
│   │   └── .fmd.toml           (config snapshot)
│   └── .cache/                 (workspace-level caches)
└── deployment-backup/
    └── release_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS/

items.title: Workspace Root
items.text:
The workspace is the top-level directory containing all releases and persistent data.

Location: ~/frappe/sites/<site>/workspace/

Created by: fmd release configure <site>

items.title: frappe-bench Symlink
items.text:
workspace/frappe-bench → release_20260416_143022

The active release is always pointed to by this symlink. Switching releases is just updating this symlink atomically.

Why symlinks?

  • Instant switching (atomic operation)
  • Zero downtime
  • Easy rollback (just point to previous release)
  • Frappe Manager expects a frappe-bench directory

items.title: deployment-data/
items.text:
Persistent data that survives across releases.

deployment-data/
├── sites/
│   └── <site.localhost>/
│       ├── site_config.json
│       ├── private/
│       ├── public/
│       └── locks/
├── config/
│   └── supervisor.conf
└── logs/
    ├── web.log
    ├── worker.log
    └── redis.log

items.title: sites/
items.text:
Frappe site data:

  • site_config.json: Site configuration (database credentials, Redis config)
  • private/: Uploaded files (attachments, backups)
  • public/: Public files (built assets served by nginx)
  • locks/: Frappe locking mechanism

This directory is symlinked from each release:

release_20260416_143022/sites → ../deployment-data/sites

items.title: config/
items.text: Supervisor configuration files for managing background workers.

items.title: logs/
items.text:
Application logs from all services:

  • web.log: Gunicorn web server logs
  • worker.log: Background worker logs
  • redis.log: Redis logs (if local Redis)

items.title: Release Directories
items.text:
Each release is a timestamped directory: release_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS

release_20260416_143022/
├── apps/
│   ├── frappe/
│   ├── erpnext/
│   └── ...
├── env/
│   └── (Python virtualenv)
├── .uv/
│   └── (UV cache)
├── .fnm/
│   └── (Node.js via fnm)
├── sites → ../deployment-data/sites
├── .fmd.toml
├── .build_log
└── .migrate_log

items.title: apps/
items.text: Source code for all installed apps. Cloned from GitHub during build.

items.title: env/
items.text:
Python virtual environment scoped to this release. Each release has its own isolated dependencies.

Created by: uv venv during release build

items.title: .uv/
items.text: UV package cache for faster subsequent dependency installs.

items.title: .fnm/
items.text: Node.js runtime managed by fnm. Each release can have a different Node version.

items.title: sites/ (symlink)
items.text: Points to ../deployment-data/sites so all releases share the same site data.

items.title: .fmd.toml
items.text: Snapshot of the configuration used to create this release. Useful for auditing and rollback.

items.title: .build_log, .migrate_log
items.text: Build and migration logs for this release.

items.title: .cache/
items.text:
Workspace-level caches shared across releases:

.cache/
├── repos/          # Git repository clones (reused across releases)
└── pip/            # Pip download cache

These caches speed up subsequent builds by avoiding re-downloading dependencies.

items.title: deployment-backup/
items.text:
Backups of releases before major operations.

deployment-backup/
└── release_20260415_120000/
    └── (full copy of release before it was deleted/modified)

Created when:

  • Deleting old releases beyond retention limit
  • Rolling back from failed deployment
  • Running fmd cleanup

items.title: Example: Multiple Releases
items.text:
After several deployments:

workspace/
├── frappe-bench → release_20260416_143022  (current)
├── deployment-data/
│   └── sites/
├── release_20260415_120000/  (old)
├── release_20260415_183045/  (old)
├── release_20260416_091532/  (previous)
├── release_20260416_143022/  (current)
└── .cache/

items.title: Disk Space Usage
items.text:
Typical release size (Frappe + ERPNext):

  • Apps source: ~150 MB
  • Python venv: ~300 MB
  • Node modules: ~200 MB
  • Built assets: ~50 MB
  • Total per release: ~700 MB

With default retention (7 releases): ~5 GB

Plus persistent data (sites/):

  • Database: depends on usage
  • Uploaded files: depends on usage
  • Backups: depends on retention policy

Recommendation: 10+ GB free space for comfortable operation.

items.title: Cleanup
items.text:
Remove old releases to free disk space:

fmd cleanup mysite.localhost -r 3 -y

This keeps the 3 most recent releases and deletes the rest.

items.title: Symlink Management
items.text:
fmd manages symlinks automatically:

  1. During configure: Creates initial frappe-bench symlink
  2. During switch: Updates symlink atomically to new release
  3. During rollback: Reverts symlink to previous release

You should never manually edit these symlinks.

items.title: Monorepo Apps
items.text:
Apps with symlink = true are symlinked instead of copied:

release_20260416_143022/apps/
├── frappe/         (copied)
├── erpnext/        (copied)
└── my-app → /path/to/monorepo/apps/my-app  (symlinked)

See Monorepo Apps Guide for details.

items.title: Next Steps
items.text:
• Understand release lifecycle
  • Learn about rollback and recovery
  • Configure retention limits

items.title: Troubleshooting
items.text: Common fmd-specific issues and solutions.

items.title: Private Repository Access
items.text:
Problem: Can't clone private repos 

ERROR: Repository access denied

Solution: Set GitHub token in config or environment 

# Via config file
github_token = "ghp_xxx"

# Via environment
export GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_xxx
fmd deploy pull --config site.toml

# Test token
curl -H "Authorization: token $GITHUB_TOKEN" https://api.github.com/user

Token requires repo scope for private repositories.

items.title: Invalid TOML Syntax
items.text:
Problem: Config validation fails with cryptic errors

Solution: Validate TOML syntax 

# Use Python TOML parser
python3 -c "import tomllib; tomllib.load(open('site.toml', 'rb'))"

# Common issues:
# - Missing quotes: repo = frappe/frappe  # ❌ Wrong
#                   repo = "frappe/frappe" # ✅ Correct
# - Unescaped backslashes in paths (Windows)
# - Invalid TOML tables: [[apps] instead of [[apps]]

items.title: Frappe Cloud Integration Failures
items.text:
Problem: FC sync fails with auth errors

Solution: Verify API credentials and site name 

# Test FC API access
curl -u fc_key:fc_secret \
  https://frappecloud.com/api/method/press.api.bench.apps

# Check team/site names match exactly
[fc]
site_name = "mysite.frappe.cloud"  # Must match FC site name
team_name = "my-team"              # Must match FC team slug

FC integration requires ALL three fields: api_key, api_secret, site_name (and optionally team_name).

items.title: Release Creation Fails
items.text:
Problem: Git clone or dependency install errors

Solutions: 1. Check network and GitHub access: 

curl -I https://github.com
git ls-remote https://github.com/frappe/frappe.git

  1. Verify app ref exists: 

    # For branch/tag
git ls-remote --heads --tags https://github.com/frappe/frappe.git | grep version-15

  2. Check system dependencies: 

    # Ubuntu/Debian
sudo apt install python3-dev build-essential mariadb-client libmariadb-dev

# macOS
brew install mariadb-connector-c

items.title: Symlink App Not Found
items.text:
Problem: Monorepo app deployment fails 

ERROR: Subdir path 'apps/my-app' not found in repo

Solution: Verify subdir path and symlink config 

[[apps]]
repo = "my-org/monorepo"
ref = "main"
subdir_path = "apps/my-app"  # Must exist in repo at this ref
symlink = true               # Required for subdir apps

Check repo structure matches: 

# Clone and verify path exists
git clone https://github.com/my-org/monorepo.git /tmp/test
ls /tmp/test/apps/my-app  # Should show app files

items.title: Database Migration Timeout
items.text:
Problem: Migration exceeds timeout during switch

Solution: Increase migration timeout 

[switch]
migrate_timeout = 600  # 10 minutes (default: 300)

For very large DBs: Migrate manually before switch, then set migrate = false.

items.title: Worker Drain Timeout
items.text:
Problem: Workers don't finish jobs within timeout

Solution: Adjust drain timeouts 

[switch]
drain_workers = true
drain_workers_timeout = 900  # 15 min (default: 300)
skip_stale_timeout = 30      # Wait 30s for stale workers
worker_kill_timeout = 30     # Force kill after 30s

For critical background jobs: Manually verify workers finished before deploying: 

# FM mode
docker exec -it <container> supervisorctl status

# Host mode
supervisorctl status | grep rq

items.title: Frappe Manager Not Found
items.text:
Problem: fmd can't find FM installation 

ERROR: frappe_manager package not found

Solution: Install Frappe Manager 

pip install frappe-manager
fm --version
fm list

fmd requires FM for Docker container management.

items.title: Container Connection Issues
items.text:
Problem: Can't connect to FM site container

Solutions: 1. Verify site running: 

fm list
fm logs site.localhost

  1. Check Docker: 

    docker ps
sudo systemctl status docker

  2. Restart site: 

    fm stop site.localhost
fm start site.localhost

items.title: Remote Worker Ports Not Exposed
items.text:
Problem: Remote worker can't connect to Redis/MariaDB

Solution: Enable remote worker mode 

# Exposes ports 3306 (MariaDB) and 6379 (Redis)
fmd remote-worker enable site.localhost --rw-server 192.168.1.100 --force

# Verify docker-compose
cat ~/frappe/sites/site.localhost/docker-compose.yml | grep ports

Without --force, only adds config without restarting containers.

items.title: Bench Not Found
items.text:
Problem: Can't locate bench directory

Solution: Verify bench_path or create new bench 

# Check if bench exists
ls -la ~/frappe-bench/apps
cat ~/frappe-bench/apps.txt

# Create new bench (if needed)
bench init --frappe-branch version-15 ~/frappe-bench

In config: bench_path is mutually exclusive with FM mode.

items.title: Python Version Mismatch
items.text:
Problem: Bench requires different Python version

Solution: Install correct Python and set explicitly 

[release]
python_version = "3.11"  # Must match system Python


# Ubuntu/Debian
sudo apt install python3.11 python3.11-dev python3.11-venv

# macOS
brew install python@3.11

fmd requires Python 3.10+ (not 3.8 as old docs claimed).

items.title: UV Installation Fails
items.text:
Problem: UV download or execution errors

Solution: fmd auto-downloads UV, but check system compatibility 

# Verify UV installed
ls -la .venv/bin/uv

# Manual install
curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh

fmd always uses UV for package installs (fallback to pip on UV failure).

items.title: Release Switch Fails Mid-Deployment
items.text:
Problem: Error during fmd release switch

Recovery: 1. Symlink still points to old release (switch is atomic via symlink, only changes on success) 2. Check current release: 

fmd release list site.localhost
readlink ~/frappe/sites/site.localhost/workspace/frappe-bench
3. Manual rollback (if needed): 
fmd release switch site.localhost release_20250409_150000

Automatic rollback: Set rollback = true in [switch] config for auto-rollback on switch failure.

items.title: Restore from Backup
items.text:
Problem: Deployment corrupted database

Solution: Manual restore from deployment-backup 

# Find backup
ls -lt ~/frappe/sites/site.localhost/deployment-backup/

# Restore manually
cd ~/frappe/sites/site.localhost/workspace/frappe-bench
bench --site site.localhost restore /path/to/backup.sql.gz

Automatic backups: Enabled via backups = true in [switch] config (default: true).

items.title: Slow App Cloning
items.text:
Problem: Git clones take forever

Solutions: 1. Check network: 

speedtest-cli

  1. Use shallow clone (future feature, not yet implemented)

  2. Pre-clone locally and use file:// URL: 

    [[apps]]
repo = "file:///tmp/frappe-cache"
ref = "version-15"

items.title: High Memory During Install
items.text:
Problem: UV/pip consumes excessive memory

Solutions: 1. Increase swap: 

sudo fallocate -l 2G /swapfile
sudo chmod 600 /swapfile
sudo mkswap /swapfile
sudo swapon /swapfile

  1. Monitor resources: 
    htop
docker stats  # For FM mode

items.title: Enable Verbose Output
items.text:
fmd -v deploy pull site.localhost  # Global -v before subcommand
fmd -v release switch site.localhost release_20250410_120000

Verbose mode shows: - Git clone commands - Dependency install output - Bench command execution - Migration logs

items.title: Check Release State
items.text:
# List all releases
fmd release list site.localhost

# Show current symlink
readlink ~/frappe/sites/site.localhost/workspace/frappe-bench

# Inspect specific release
ls -la ~/frappe/sites/site.localhost/workspace/release_20250410_120000/
cat ~/frappe/sites/site.localhost/workspace/release_20250410_120000/.fmd.toml

items.title: Verify Config
items.text:
# Show effective config (after overrides)
fmd info site.localhost --config site.toml

# Validate TOML
python3 -c "import tomllib; print(tomllib.load(open('site.toml', 'rb')))"

items.title: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'frappe_manager'
items.text:
Cause: Frappe Manager not installed

Fix: pip install frappe-manager

items.title: ERROR: bench_path and FM mode are mutually exclusive
items.text:
Cause: Config has both bench_path set AND trying to use FM mode

Fix: Remove bench_path from config for FM mode, or set bench_path for host mode (one or the other, not both)

items.title: ERROR: Site not found in Frappe Manager
items.text:
Cause: FM site doesn't exist yet

Fix: Create site first: 

fm create site.localhost
# Then deploy
fmd deploy pull site.localhost --config site.toml

items.title: ERROR: Python 3.9 not supported
items.text:
Cause: fmd requires Python 3.10+

Fix: Upgrade Python: 

# Ubuntu/Debian
sudo apt install python3.10 python3.10-venv

# macOS
brew install python@3.10

items.title: ERROR: use_fc_apps enabled but no FC credentials
items.text:
Cause: FC integration enabled without credentials

Fix: Add FC config section: 

[release]
use_fc_apps = true

[fc]
api_key = "fc_xxx"
api_secret = "fc_xxx"
site_name = "mysite.frappe.cloud"

items.title: Collect Debug Information
items.text:
When reporting issues, include:

# 1. fmd version
fmd --version

# 2. Python version
python3 --version

# 3. OS info
uname -a

# 4. Full command with verbose output
fmd -v deploy pull site.localhost 2>&1 | tee debug.log

# 5. Config file (remove tokens)
cat site.toml | grep -v token | grep -v api_key

# 6. Release state
fmd release list site.localhost
